mnemonics: C++ TEMPLATES: Container (C) for Every (E) My (M) Preferred (P) Language (L) Type (T) Securely (S)
keyPoints: Generic classes use templates to create data structures that work with any data type (e.g., vector<T>, list<T>)
The 'typename' keyword is used to declare a type parameter in templates and handle dependent types
The 'export' keyword (largely deprecated/unused in modern C++) was intended to allow separate compilation of template definitions
commonMistakes: Forgetting to include the template definition in the same file where it's used (violating the One Definition Rule)
Using incorrect syntax when specializing templates or forgetting the 'template<>' prefix
Attempting to separate template declaration and definition without proper implementation (the export keyword doesn't work in practice)
